title: althaea plebeia
intro: Althaea plebeia, also known as the desert mallow, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Malvaceae and is native to various parts of the world, including the Middle East and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which are attributed to its mucilages, a type of complex carbohydrate that forms a protective gel-like barrier on the mucous membranes. The medicinal properties of Althaea plebeia make it beneficial for treating digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome and ulcers, as well as skin conditions like eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of Althaea plebeia can be made into teas, tinctures, or creams, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its health benefits.
